description: T617 FlowCanvasWriter MUST enforce: BOLA at ORDER 1 (flow.tenantId === ALS.tenantId), FLOW_IMMUTABLE guard at ORDER 2 (status !== PUBLISHED), canvas write at ORDER 3 (DRAFT only), storeDocument(audit) before FlowCanvasUpdated (DNA-8), FlowImmutableRejected on published flow write.
blockConditions: T617 does not check BOLA at ORDER 1 before FLOW_IMMUTABLE
T617 allows write to PUBLISHED flows
T617 checks FLOW_IMMUTABLE after canvas write — partial write to published flow
T617 calls enqueue(FlowCanvasUpdated) before storeDocument(audit)
T617 returns DataProcessResult.failure without emitting FlowImmutableRejected
passConditions: T617 validates flow.tenantId === ALS.tenantId at ORDER 1
T617 checks flow.status !== PUBLISHED at ORDER 2 — FLOW_IMMUTABLE_STATES compile-time const
T617 writes canvas at ORDER 3 only after DRAFT confirmed
T617 storeDocument(audit) before enqueue(FlowCanvasUpdated) — DNA-8
FlowImmutableRejected emitted on published flow write attempt

description: T617 FlowCanvasWriter MUST prevent writes to published flows. DRAFT→PUBLISHED is a one-way state transition in the visual flow engine domain. Published flows represent deployed configurations that must not be altered retroactively.
blockConditions: T617 allows updateDocument on PUBLISHED flows
T617 treats PUBLISHED as just another writable state
T617 allows operator bypass to modify published flows
passConditions: T617 rejects all writes to flows with status PUBLISHED
FlowImmutableRejected emitted with current status on published write attempt
FLOW_IMMUTABLE_STATES = ['PUBLISHED'] defined as compile-time constant

description: T617 FlowCanvasWriter MUST validate that the requesting tenant owns the flow canvas (BOLA). A different tenant must not be able to overwrite another tenant's flow definition, which would allow cross-tenant data corruption.
blockConditions: T617 allows a non-owner tenant to write to a flow canvas
T617 accepts flowTenantId from request payload instead of comparing to ALS
T617 trusts tenantId from event body for BOLA comparison
passConditions: T617 fetches flow from database and compares flow.tenantId to ALS.tenantId
Mismatch → BOLA_VIOLATION rejection; FlowCanvasUpdateFailed emitted with BOLA_VIOLATION
BOLA comparison uses ALS tenantId — never from payload

description: T617 FlowCanvasWriter MUST check FLOW_IMMUTABLE guard before any write attempt. A post-write immutable check creates a TOCTOU window where a published flow can receive partial writes before the check fails.
blockConditions: T617 writes canvas data and then checks if PUBLISHED — TOCTOU window
T617 reads immutable state from FREEDOM config — runtime-configurable immutability is attackable
T617 allows PUBLISHED state override via admin API
passConditions: T617 checks flow.status against FLOW_IMMUTABLE_STATES at ORDER 2, before any write
FLOW_IMMUTABLE_STATES is a compile-time constant — not from database or config
No runtime path can override FLOW_IMMUTABLE enforcement

description: T617 FlowCanvasWriter MUST emit FlowCanvasUpdated (success), FlowImmutableRejected (published write), and FlowCanvasUpdateFailed (BOLA violation). All three event types required for downstream consumer completeness.
blockConditions: T617 success path does not emit FlowCanvasUpdated
T617 PUBLISHED write does not emit FlowImmutableRejected
T617 BOLA violation does not emit FlowCanvasUpdateFailed
passConditions: FlowCanvasUpdated emitted with flowId, tenantId, updatedAt on success
FlowImmutableRejected emitted with flowId, status on PUBLISHED write attempt
FlowCanvasUpdateFailed emitted with flowId, reason BOLA_VIOLATION on ownership failure

description: T617 FlowCanvasWriter MUST use IFlowCanvasService (F1547) for BOLA check + state check + canvas CRUD, and ICanvasAuditService (F1548) for audit trail. Direct database access without fabric interfaces violates Rule 1.
blockConditions: T617 imports database SDK directly instead of using fabric interfaces
T617 performs BOLA check without F1547 IFlowCanvasService
T617 writes audit without using F1548 ICanvasAuditService
passConditions: T617 injects F1547 IFlowCanvasService for flow canvas lookup, BOLA, and CRUD
T617 injects F1548 ICanvasAuditService for canvas write audit trail
All canvas reads and writes routed through F1547 — no direct database access

description: T617 generation prompt MUST include: BOLA_OWNERSHIP_CHECK at ORDER 1, FLOW_IMMUTABLE_STATE_GUARD at ORDER 2, DRAFT_ONLY_WRITE at ORDER 3, DNA-8 audit before FlowCanvasUpdated. FLOW_IMMUTABLE_STATES compile-time constant directive.
blockConditions: Generation prompt omits BOLA check directive
Generation prompt specifies FLOW_IMMUTABLE as runtime-configurable
Generation prompt does not specify write prohibition on PUBLISHED flows

Generation prompt includes FLOW_IMMUTABLE_STATE_GUARD at ORDER 2 — compile-time const
Generation prompt specifies DRAFT_ONLY_WRITE at ORDER 3
Generation prompt specifies DNA-8 storeDocument(audit) before FlowCanvasUpdated

description: T618 FlowPublicationOrchestrator MUST enforce: DFS cycle detection (WHITE/GRAY/BLACK) at ORDER 1, type compatibility per-edge at ORDER 2, OCC updateDocument(PUBLISHED) at ORDER 3, storeDocument(audit) before FlowPublished (DNA-8), CycleDetected + TypeMismatch + FlowPublicationConflict on respective failures.
blockConditions: T618 publishes without cycle detection
T618 runs type compatibility before cycle detection
T618 uses plain storeDocument instead of OCC for publication
T618 emits FlowPublished before storeDocument(audit)
T618 does not emit CycleDetected when cycle found
passConditions: T618 runs DFS WHITE/GRAY/BLACK cycle detection at ORDER 1 before any type check
T618 validates type compatibility per-edge at ORDER 2 after cycle detection
T618 uses OCC version check for DRAFT→PUBLISHED transition at ORDER 3
T618 storeDocument(audit) before FlowPublished enqueue — DNA-8
CycleDetected, TypeMismatch, FlowPublicationConflict each emitted on their respective failures

description: T618 FlowPublicationOrchestrator MUST prevent cyclic flow graphs from being published. A flow with a cycle would create an infinite execution loop at runtime. Cycle detection is a hard safety gate — no publication without acyclic confirmation.
blockConditions: T618 publishes a flow graph that contains a cycle
T618 treats cycle detection as a warning rather than a blocking gate
T618 allows publication after cycle detection failure if operator overrides
passConditions: T618 DFS detects cycles before any write or type check
CycleDetected emitted immediately on cycle found — no further validation or write
Publication blocked until graph is acyclic — no operator override path

description: T618 FlowPublicationOrchestrator MUST validate type compatibility per-edge before publication. A type mismatch between connected nodes would cause runtime data corruption when the flow executes. Schema-driven edge validation is a pre-deployment safety check.
blockConditions: T618 publishes without checking edge type compatibility
T618 uses string equality for schema comparison — misses structural matches
T618 skips type check for edges without explicit type annotations
passConditions: T618 validates sourceOutputType against targetInputType for every edge
TypeMismatch emitted with edge identifier and schema details on incompatibility
Type check uses schema-driven comparison — all edges with type annotations validated

description: T618 FlowPublicationOrchestrator MUST use OCC for DRAFT→PUBLISHED transition. Plain storeDocument without version check allows double-publication race — two concurrent publication requests both succeed, creating version inconsistency.
blockConditions: T618 uses plain storeDocument for publication — no version check
T618 allows publication without expectedVersion parameter
T618 does not emit FlowPublicationConflict on version mismatch
passConditions: T618 reads currentVersion from database before publication
T618 compares expectedVersion to currentVersion — OCC_CONFLICT on mismatch
FlowPublicationConflict emitted with expectedVersion and actualVersion on conflict

description: T618 FlowPublicationOrchestrator MUST emit FlowPublished (success), CycleDetected (cycle), TypeMismatch (edge incompatibility), FlowPublicationConflict (OCC conflict). All 4 event types required for downstream completeness.
blockConditions: T618 success path does not emit FlowPublished

FlowPublicationConflict emitted with expectedVersion, actualVersion on OCC conflict

description: T618 FlowPublicationOrchestrator MUST use IFlowCanvasService (F1547) for canvas read + OCC publish write and IFlowPublicationAuditService (F1549) for audit trail. DFS cycle detection algorithm must be implemented in the service, not outsourced to a third-party graph library.

description: T618 generation prompt MUST include: DFS_CYCLE_DETECTION_WHITE_GRAY_BLACK at ORDER 1, TYPE_COMPATIBILITY_PER_EDGE at ORDER 2, OCC_DRAFT_TO_PUBLISHED at ORDER 3, DNA-8 audit before FlowPublished.

description: T619 NodeTypeRegistrar MUST enforce: SETNX at ORDER 1 before either write, storeDocument(nodeType) at ORDER 2, storeDocument(capabilityIndex) at ORDER 3, redis.del(lockKey) in catch block on write failure, storeDocument(audit) before NodeTypeRegistered (DNA-8).

T619 writes capabilityIndex before nodeType (wrong dual-write order)
T619 does not call redis.del(lockKey) in catch block on write failure
T619 emits NodeTypeRegistered before storeDocument(audit)
T619 leaves lock held on write failure — hung lock state

NodeTypeAlreadyExists emitted when lock is already held

description: T619 NodeTypeRegistrar MUST prevent duplicate node type registrations. Each nodeTypeId must be unique in the system. SETNX ensures that concurrent registration requests for the same nodeTypeId result in only one successful registration.
blockConditions: T619 allows two concurrent registrations for the same nodeTypeId to both succeed
T619 does not check for existing lock before writing nodeType
T619 uses conditional write (upsert) instead of SETNX — different semantics
passConditions: T619 queries SETNX lock before writing — lock held means already registered or registering

Only one NodeTypeRegistered event emitted per nodeTypeId regardless of concurrent requests

description: T619 NodeTypeRegistrar MUST perform atomic dual-write: both nodeType record and capabilityIndex entry must exist together or neither. A nodeType without a capability index entry leaves the system in an inconsistent state where the node type exists but cannot be discovered by capability queries.
blockConditions: T619 writes nodeType but skips capabilityIndex on success
T619 writes capabilityIndex before nodeType — wrong order
T619 does not have atomic consistency between nodeType and capabilityIndex
passConditions: T619 writes nodeType at ORDER 2 and capabilityIndex at ORDER 3 in sequence
redis.del(lockKey) in catch block rolls back lock if either write fails
Both nodeType record and capabilityIndex entry always exist together after success

description: T619 NodeTypeRegistrar MUST call redis.del(lockKey) in the catch block to prevent hung lock state. A hung lock permanently blocks future registrations for the same nodeTypeId — an attacker could exploit this by triggering a registration failure and leaving the lock held.
blockConditions: T619 does not call redis.del(lockKey) in catch — lock remains held on write failure
T619 only clears lock on successful registration — failures leave locks permanently
T619 uses lock TTL only — TTL expiry is too slow for high-frequency registration scenarios
passConditions: T619 catch block calls deleteDocument(NODE_TYPE_LOCKS_INDEX, lockKey) immediately on failure
Lock release is best-effort (catch on deleteDocument too) — no cascading failures
Future registrations of same nodeTypeId unblocked immediately after lock release

description: T619 NodeTypeRegistrar MUST emit NodeTypeRegistered (success), NodeTypeAlreadyExists (lock held), and NodeTypeRegistrationFailed (write failure). All 3 event types required for downstream completeness.

T619 lock-held path does not emit NodeTypeAlreadyExists
T619 write failure path does not emit NodeTypeRegistrationFailed
passConditions: NodeTypeRegistered emitted with nodeTypeId, tenantId, registeredAt on success

description: T620 CodeInjectionProcessor MUST enforce: version lock at ORDER 1, pre-write audit (rollback pointer) storeDocument at ORDER 2 BEFORE injection, inject at ORDER 3, append-only result audit at ORDER 4, storeDocument(audit) before CodeInjected (DNA-8). No updateDocument on any injection audit record.

description: T620 CodeInjectionProcessor MUST prevent concurrent injection of the same node version. Two concurrent AI-generated code injections for the same node+version would overwrite each other, producing non-deterministic code state.

T620 does not check for existing version lock before injection

description: T620 CodeInjectionProcessor MUST store the pre-write audit BEFORE injection to create a rollback pointer. If injection fails after the pre-write audit is stored, the audit record provides evidence of the attempted injection and can be used to roll back to the pre-injection state.
blockConditions: T620 stores audit only after successful injection — no audit record if injection fails
T620 stores pre-write audit but makes it mutable — can be altered to hide failed injections
T620 omits PRE_WRITE phase and only records COMPLETE or FAILED outcomes
passConditions: T620 storeDocument(phase:PRE_WRITE) at ORDER 2 before any injection code runs
PRE_WRITE audit exists even when injection fails — complete audit trail
PRE_WRITE audit is append-only — cannot be modified after creation

description: T620 CodeInjectionProcessor MUST maintain an append-only injection audit extending NON-REPUDIATION-AUDIT-001. AI-generated code injections are a high-security operation — the audit trail must be non-repudiable to support security review and forensic analysis.
blockConditions: T620 calls updateDocument on injection audit records — audit trail corrupted
T620 combines all phases in single mutable record — one compromise invalidates trail
T620 omits injection audit entirely — no non-repudiable evidence
passConditions: T620 uses storeDocument only for all injection audit writes — no updateDocument
T620 INJECTION_AUDIT_PHASES = ['PRE_WRITE','COMPLETE','FAILED'] compile-time constant
T620 extends NON-REPUDIATION-AUDIT-001 from FLOW-16 — each phase is independent storeDocument

description: T620 CodeInjectionProcessor MUST emit CodeInjected (success), InjectionConflict (lock held), and InjectionFailed (injection failure). All 3 event types required for downstream completeness. All 3 audit phases (PRE_WRITE, COMPLETE, FAILED) must be stored.
